pattern: A word can be dropped when it is a suffix of another word, so removing every word that ends another and summing the survivors plus their terminators gives the shortest encoding.
hints: One word costs nothing extra when it is exactly the tail of a longer word already in the list.
Insert each word reversed into a trie; only the words that become a full root-to-leaf path (no word extends them) need their own slot.
Discard any word that is a suffix of another, then the answer is the sum over the remaining words of their length plus one for the terminator.

edge_inputs.description: first example, one word is a suffix of another
chain of nested suffixes collapses to one word
no word is a suffix of another
two words share a suffix but neither contains the other
a word that is a prefix, not a suffix, is not absorbed

stress.description: random short lowercase words with frequent shared suffixes
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 2000))
    words = set()
    for _ in range(n):
        ln = rng.randint(1, 7)
        w = ''.join(rng.choice('abc') for _ in range(ln))
        words.add(w)
    return [list(words)]
stress.note: small alphabet maximises suffix overlaps to stress absorption
